zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Curtice於1626年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆時，他的父親Richard Curtice32歲，母親Sarah Anne Emery28歲。他於1645年與Sarah Carwithy在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1671年3月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 約克 可隆尼素福爾可素爾德享年45歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Curtice于1626年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆时，他的父亲Richard Curtice32岁，母亲Sarah Anne Emery28岁。他于1645年与Sarah Carwithy在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可薩萊姆结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1671年3月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 約克 可隆尼素福爾可素爾德享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: Curtice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Possibly an altered form of North German   Gerdes  .

variants.name: Curtis
meanings: English: nickname for a refined person, sometimes perhaps given ironically, from Middle English, Old French courteis, courtois, curtis ‘courtly, refined, urbane’ (derivative of Old French court; see Court 1).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.description: After the English took over in the 1660s, the colony was renamed New York, after the Duke of York

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Curtice was born in 1626, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, his father, Richard Curtice, was 32 and his mother, Sarah Anne Emery, was 28. He married Sarah Carwithy in 1645, in Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died in March 1671, in Southold, Suffolk, New York Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 45.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Curticeが1626年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスサレムで生まれた時，父Richard Curticeは32歳，母Sarah Anne Emeryは28歳であった。1645年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーエセックスサレムでSarah Carwithyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1671年3月にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ヨーク コロニースフォルクソウソルドで45歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Curtice(이)가 1626년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살렘에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Curtice(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah Anne Emery(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1645년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 살렘에서 Sarah Carwithy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1671년 3월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이 수폴크 소우토드에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Curtice nasceu em 1626, em Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, seu pai, Richard Curtice, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Anne Emery, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Carwithy em 1645, em Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em março de 1671, em Southold, Suffolk, New York Colony, British Colonial America, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Curtice родился в 1626 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Richard Curtice было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Sarah Anne Emery, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Carwithy в 1645 г. в местности сеилем, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер марта 1671 г. в местности соутолд, суффолк, ню ерк колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Curtice nació en 1626, en Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, Richard Curtice, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Sarah Anne Emery, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Sarah Carwithy en 1645, en Salem, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en marzo de 1671, en Southold, Suffolk, New York Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 45 años.
